LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MARCH-14-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT GRANTS THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO APPROVE THE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. The court will enter the proposed judgment lodged with this filing.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
